landscaping.label: Landscaping
scopeItems: Complete a pre-start walkthrough to confirm boundaries, access, measurements, and visible site conditions.
Protect nearby surfaces, planting areas, paths, and lawn sections that are not part of the agreed work.
Prepare beds, borders, lawn edges, planting zones, and visible work areas according to the project details.
Supply labor, standard tools, and agreed materials needed to complete the listed landscaping work.
Install, shape, tidy, and finish the agreed landscaping elements with attention to drainage, levels, and presentation.
Complete a final tidy, remove routine green waste, and walk through the finished work with the client.
paymentTerms: A deposit may be requested to reserve the schedule and cover upfront plant or material costs.
Progress payments may apply for multi-day work or material-heavy projects.
Remaining balance is due on practical completion unless different terms are agreed in writing.
assumptions: Client will provide safe access to outdoor work areas during agreed working hours.
The proposal assumes normal soil conditions and no concealed waste, rubble, services, or drainage defects.
Major grading, retaining work, tree surgery, stump grinding, or root removal are excluded unless listed in the project details.
Plant availability may require like-for-like substitutions of similar quality, size, and style.
Ongoing watering, plant establishment, and seasonal maintenance remain the client's responsibility unless added as a service.

paving.label: Paving
scopeItems: Confirm layout, dimensions, access route, drainage direction, and finish selection before work begins.
Mark out the work area and protect nearby thresholds, walls, lawns, beds, and existing surfaces where practical.
Prepare the area, including excavation, disposal, sub-base preparation, and compaction described in the project details.
Install base layers, edge restraints, bedding, and paving finish using suitable trade methods for the selected surface.
Cut, align, joint, compact, and finish paving so the completed surface is neat, stable, and ready for normal use.
Remove routine installation debris and complete a final handover check with the client.
paymentTerms: A deposit may be required before ordering materials or reserving installation dates.
A progress payment may be requested once excavation/base preparation is complete on larger projects.
Final payment is due once installation is complete and the client has received the finished proposal scope.
assumptions: Quote assumes normal access for materials, tools, and waste removal.
The existing ground is assumed suitable for standard preparation unless otherwise stated in the project details.
Drainage changes, retaining work, major excavation, utility relocation, or remedial structural work are excluded unless stated.
Color variation, texture variation, and minor shade differences in natural or manufactured paving products are expected.
Client approvals for pattern, border, and finish selection are required before installation begins.

cleaning.label: Cleaning
scopeItems: Confirm property access, priority rooms, surface sensitivities, and any areas that should not be touched before starting.
Prepare the work area by identifying fragile items, access constraints, and any visible pre-existing damage.
Complete the cleaning tasks described in the project details using suitable products, equipment, and safe working methods.
Dust, wipe, vacuum, mop, sanitize, and detail agreed areas according to the selected service level.
Check completed rooms or work zones for missed items, streaking, residue, or obvious touch-up needs before handover.
Report access issues, damage noticed on arrival, or out-of-scope requests promptly.
paymentTerms: Payment is due on completion unless a recurring service agreement is in place.
Recurring services may be invoiced weekly, biweekly, or monthly by written agreement.
Cancellations, lockouts, or access issues inside the agreed notice period may be billable.
assumptions: Client will provide safe access, running water, electricity, and any required entry instructions.
Hazardous waste, pest activity, biohazards, and specialist restoration are excluded unless agreed separately.
Heavy furniture moving, appliance disassembly, and high-level exterior work are excluded unless stated in the project details.
Cleaning results depend on surface age, staining, mineral deposits, wear, and prior product buildup.
Client will secure valuables, confidential documents, pets, and fragile items before the appointment.

general-contractor.label: General Contractor
scopeItems: Confirm the final work area, client requirements, site access, material selections, and schedule before work begins.
Coordinate labor, standard tools, agreed materials, and any planned trade handoffs needed for the listed work.
Protect adjacent finishes and occupied areas where practical before active work begins.
Complete the project tasks described in the job details using appropriate trade practices and manufacturer guidance where applicable.
Maintain a reasonably tidy and safe work area throughout the project, including routine debris management.
Walk through completed work with the client and identify any agreed punch-list items before final handover.
paymentTerms: A deposit or staged payment may be required for scheduling, labor planning, and materials.
Change orders, added materials, and out-of-scope work must be approved before they are started.
Final balance is due when the agreed scope is complete unless a written payment schedule says otherwise.
assumptions: Permits, drawings, engineering, and inspections are excluded unless specifically included in the project details.
Hidden damage, structural defects, mold, asbestos, or unsafe conditions may require a change order.
Client decisions, material availability, inspection windows, and third-party delays may affect the final timeline.
The proposal assumes normal working access, clear work areas, and utilities available during agreed working hours.
Finish matching depends on available materials, existing conditions, and manufacturer tolerances.

painting.label: Painting
scopeItems: Confirm colors, sheen, finish areas, access, and surface conditions before work begins.
Protect floors, fixtures, furniture, landscaping, and adjacent surfaces where practical.
Prepare agreed surfaces through routine cleaning, sanding, filling, caulking, and spot priming as required.
Apply agreed primer, paint, stain, or finish using suitable trade methods for the surface and product.
Complete touch-ups, remove routine masking, and leave finished areas neat at handover.
Walk through the completed work with the client and record any agreed touch-up items.
paymentTerms: A deposit may be required to reserve the schedule and purchase paint or finishing materials.
Progress payment may apply for multi-room, exterior, or multi-stage projects.
Final payment is due when the agreed painting scope is complete.
assumptions: Client will confirm final colors and finishes before materials are purchased.
Major drywall repair, rot repair, lead paint handling, mold remediation, or water damage repair are excluded unless stated.
Coverage may vary based on existing color, surface porosity, product choice, and lighting conditions.
Client will move or secure delicate items unless furniture moving is included in the project details.

plumbing.label: Plumbing
scopeItems: Confirm fixture locations, shutoff access, water pressure, visible pipework, and client priorities before work begins.
Protect nearby surfaces and isolate the work area where practical.
Supply standard labor, fittings, and materials listed in the project details.
Complete the agreed plumbing repair, replacement, installation, or connection using suitable trade practices.
Test visible connections for leaks and confirm basic operation before handover.
Clean routine work debris and explain any follow-up recommendations to the client.
paymentTerms: A deposit may be requested for fixtures, special-order parts, or scheduled installation work.
Additional hidden defects or code-required upgrades require approval before proceeding.
Final payment is due once the agreed plumbing scope has been completed and tested.
assumptions: Quote assumes accessible shutoffs, exposed work areas, and standard residential/commercial plumbing conditions.
Hidden pipe damage, blocked drains, failed valves, wall/floor repair, or water damage restoration are excluded unless stated.
Permits, inspections, and code upgrades are excluded unless included in the project details.
Client will provide access to water shutoffs and affected rooms during agreed working hours.

electrical.label: Electrical
scopeItems: Confirm access, fixture/device locations, panel location, circuit needs, and visible site conditions before work begins.
Protect adjacent finishes and isolate the work area where practical.
Supply standard labor, devices, fixtures, or materials listed in the project details.
Complete agreed electrical installation, replacement, troubleshooting, or repair using suitable trade practices.
Test completed devices, switches, fixtures, or circuits for basic operation before handover.
Clean routine work debris and note any safety or upgrade recommendations.
paymentTerms: A deposit may be required for fixtures, devices, panels, or special-order electrical materials.
Additional faults, unsafe wiring, or code-required upgrades require approval before proceeding.
Final payment is due once the agreed electrical scope is complete and basic operation has been tested.
assumptions: Quote assumes accessible work areas, suitable existing wiring, and available panel capacity unless stated otherwise.
Permit fees, inspections, panel upgrades, drywall repair, painting, or hidden wiring defects are excluded unless included.
Client will provide safe access to the electrical panel and affected rooms during agreed working hours.
Final compliance requirements may depend on local authority, inspector, or utility requirements.

handyman.label: Handyman
scopeItems: Confirm task list, priorities, work areas, access, and any supplied materials before work begins.
Protect nearby surfaces and organize the work area for safe, efficient completion.
Complete the listed repair, assembly, installation, adjustment, or maintenance tasks using appropriate methods.
Use standard tools and agreed materials required for the described work.
Check completed items for basic function, fit, alignment, or finish before handover.
Tidy routine debris and review completed tasks with the client.
paymentTerms: A deposit may be requested for scheduled work requiring materials or reserved time.
Additional tasks beyond the agreed list may be quoted separately or billed by approved change order.
Final payment is due when the agreed handyman scope is complete.
assumptions: Quote assumes tasks are non-structural and accessible with standard tools and ladders.
Specialist trade work, permits, hazardous materials, structural repairs, or hidden damage are excluded unless stated.
Client-supplied materials are assumed complete, suitable, and available at the start of work.
Manufacturer defects, missing parts, and incompatible hardware may affect completion or require additional costs.

pressure-washing.label: Pressure Washing
scopeItems: Confirm surfaces, water access, drainage, staining, and any delicate areas before work begins.
Protect nearby plants, outlets, fixtures, furniture, and areas that should not be washed where practical.
Prepare the surface by clearing routine loose debris from agreed work areas.
Apply suitable pressure washing or soft washing methods based on surface type and condition.
Rinse cleaned areas and review obvious remaining stains, marks, or problem areas with the client.
Tidy routine equipment and leave access areas safe for normal use.
paymentTerms: Payment is due on completion unless recurring exterior maintenance terms are agreed.
A deposit may be requested for larger commercial, multi-surface, or scheduled route work.
Additional surfaces requested on site may be quoted separately before work continues.
assumptions: Client will provide safe access to water, exterior areas, and any required gates or entry points.
Results depend on surface age, staining, oxidation, algae, prior coatings, and material condition.
Paint removal, restoration, sealing, hazardous cleanup, or repairs are excluded unless stated.
Client will move vehicles, fragile decor, and loose outdoor items before the appointment.

roofing.label: Roofing
scopeItems: Confirm roof access, visible issue areas, material type, weather constraints, and safety requirements before work begins.
Protect surrounding areas where practical and prepare safe access for agreed roofing work.
Supply labor, standard tools, and agreed materials listed in the project details.
Complete agreed repair, replacement, flashing, sealing, inspection, or maintenance work using suitable trade practices.
Remove routine roofing debris and check visible completed areas before handover.
Review completed work, warranty notes, and any recommended follow-up items with the client.
paymentTerms: A deposit may be required for roofing materials, scheduling, and setup.
Progress payments may apply for multi-day or material-heavy roofing projects.
Final payment is due when the agreed roofing scope is complete, subject to weather and safe access.
assumptions: Quote assumes safe roof access and no concealed structural damage unless stated in the project details.
Decking replacement, major structural repair, insulation, interior damage, mold, or pest issues are excluded unless included.
Weather, supplier availability, and safety conditions may affect the schedule.
Permits, inspections, and disposal fees are excluded unless specifically included.

hvac.label: HVAC
hvac.overviewLead: heating, ventilation, and cooling service project
scopeItems: Confirm equipment type, thermostat location, access, airflow concerns, and client priorities before work begins.
Protect nearby flooring, walls, fixtures, and occupied areas where practical.
Inspect visible equipment, connections, filters, vents, and service access related to the agreed work.
Complete the listed HVAC repair, maintenance, replacement, installation, or adjustment using suitable trade practices.
Test basic system operation, airflow, thermostat response, and visible connections before handover.
Review completed work, maintenance recommendations, and any observed follow-up items with the client.
paymentTerms: A deposit may be requested for scheduled installs, replacement equipment, or special-order parts.
Additional repairs, code-required upgrades, or hidden defects require approval before work continues.
Final payment is due once the agreed HVAC scope has been completed and basic operation is checked.
assumptions: Quote assumes safe access to equipment, electrical service, thermostat wiring, and affected rooms.
Duct redesign, electrical upgrades, refrigerant recovery, permit fees, or structural access work are excluded unless stated.
System performance depends on existing ductwork, insulation, equipment age, building conditions, and maintenance history.
Client will provide access to indoor and outdoor units during agreed working hours.

flooring.label: Flooring
scopeItems: Confirm flooring type, layout, transitions, room access, subfloor condition, and finish direction before work begins.
Protect adjacent finishes, baseboards, thresholds, and areas outside the agreed work zone where practical.
Prepare agreed surfaces through routine clearing, cleaning, minor leveling, and layout planning described in the project details.
Install, repair, refinish, or replace the listed flooring materials using suitable trade methods.
Fit trims, transitions, and visible edges included in the agreed scope.
Remove routine installation debris and complete a final walkthrough with the client.
paymentTerms: A deposit may be required before ordering flooring, trim, adhesive, underlay, or specialty materials.
Progress payments may apply for multi-room or multi-day flooring projects.
Final payment is due when the agreed flooring scope is complete.
assumptions: Quote assumes normal subfloor conditions and clear access to the work areas.
Major subfloor repair, moisture remediation, asbestos handling, baseboard replacement, or door trimming are excluded unless stated.
Color, grain, batch, and texture variation may occur in natural or manufactured flooring products.
Client will remove fragile items and confirm final material selections before ordering.

remodeling.label: Remodeling
scopeItems: Confirm final design intent, finish selections, access, work areas, and schedule before work begins.
Protect adjacent occupied areas, finishes, pathways, and surfaces where practical.
Coordinate standard labor, materials, tools, and trade handoffs needed for the listed remodeling scope.
Complete agreed demolition, preparation, installation, finishing, and adjustment tasks described in the project details.
Maintain routine debris control and a reasonably safe work area during active work.
Walk through completed work with the client and document any agreed punch-list items.
paymentTerms: A deposit or staged payment schedule may be required for materials, scheduling, and labor planning.
Change orders, upgraded materials, and added work must be approved before they are started.
Final balance is due when the agreed remodeling scope is complete unless otherwise agreed in writing.
assumptions: Permits, drawings, engineering, inspections, and design services are excluded unless specifically included.
Hidden damage, structural defects, mold, asbestos, plumbing/electrical issues, or unsafe conditions may require a change order.
Timeline may depend on client approvals, material availability, inspection windows, and third-party trade schedules.
Finish matching depends on existing conditions, available materials, and manufacturer tolerances.

moving.label: Moving
scopeItems: Confirm pickup and delivery addresses, access, inventory, stairs/elevators, parking, and timing before move day.
Protect routine traffic areas, doorways, and handled items where practical.
Load, transport, and unload agreed items using suitable moving methods for normal household or business contents.
Place items in designated rooms or areas according to client direction.
Track visible pre-existing damage or access issues noticed during the move.
Complete a final walkthrough to confirm listed items have been delivered or staged.
paymentTerms: A deposit may be requested to reserve the moving date and crew.
Additional time, extra stops, packing materials, or unlisted items may be billable with approval.
Final payment is due on completion of the agreed moving scope.
assumptions: Client will provide accurate inventory, safe access, and any building rules before move day.
Specialty items, pianos, safes, hoisting, long carries, or storage fees are excluded unless listed.
Client will pack personal items unless packing services are included.
Weather, traffic, elevator availability, parking restrictions, and building access may affect timing.

pest-control.label: Pest Control
scopeItems: Confirm pest activity, affected areas, access, safety concerns, and client observations before service begins.
Inspect visible entry points, activity areas, moisture sources, and conditions related to the agreed treatment.
Apply agreed treatment, exclusion, trapping, baiting, or monitoring methods according to the service details.
Use appropriate products, equipment, and safety practices for the listed pest concern.
Review visible findings, preparation needs, and follow-up recommendations with the client.
Document routine service notes and any areas requiring monitoring or repeat treatment.
paymentTerms: Payment is due on completion unless a recurring pest plan is in place.
Recurring service plans may be billed monthly, quarterly, or by written agreement.
Additional pests, severe infestations, or structural exclusion work may require a separate quote.
assumptions: Client will provide access to affected areas and follow preparation or re-entry instructions.
Results depend on sanitation, building conditions, neighboring activity, weather, and client follow-through.
Structural repairs, insulation removal, biohazard cleanup, and wildlife relocation are excluded unless stated.
Follow-up visits may be required depending on pest type, lifecycle, and activity level.

pool-service.label: Pool Service
scopeItems: Confirm pool type, equipment access, water condition, service history, and client priorities before work begins.
Inspect visible pool surfaces, water level, equipment, skimmers, filters, and circulation components related to the service.
Complete agreed cleaning, chemical balancing, maintenance, repair, opening, or closing tasks.
Use suitable pool products, tools, and testing methods for the listed scope.
Check visible operation of serviced equipment and note any observed follow-up needs.
Review service findings, chemical notes, and client care recommendations before handover.
paymentTerms: Payment is due on completion unless recurring pool service terms are agreed.
A deposit may be requested for parts, equipment, opening, closing, or repair work.
Additional chemicals, parts, leak diagnosis, or equipment repairs may require approval before proceeding.
assumptions: Client will provide safe access to the pool, equipment area, water source, and electrical controls.
Water clarity results depend on starting condition, weather, filtration, chemical history, and bather load.
Leak repair, resurfacing, electrical work, major plumbing repair, and equipment replacement are excluded unless stated.
Service timing may depend on weather, water condition, and equipment functionality.

appliance-repair.label: Appliance Repair
scopeItems: Confirm appliance type, model details, symptoms, access, and prior troubleshooting before work begins.
Inspect visible appliance components, connections, controls, and installation conditions related to the reported issue.
Complete agreed diagnosis, repair, replacement, adjustment, or installation tasks using suitable service methods.
Supply standard labor and parts listed in the project details or approved before installation.
Test basic appliance operation after service where safe and practical.
Review completed work, care notes, and any recommended next steps with the client.
paymentTerms: Diagnostic fees may be due at the visit and may be credited toward approved repair work if stated.
A deposit may be requested for special-order parts.
Final payment is due once the approved appliance service is complete.
assumptions: Quote assumes safe access, available utilities, and normal installation conditions.
Hidden wiring, plumbing, cabinetry, flooring, manufacturer defects, or discontinued parts may affect repair options.
Parts availability, model age, and manufacturer support may affect timeline and final cost.
Client will clear access around the appliance before the appointment.

junk-removal.label: Junk Removal
scopeItems: Confirm item list, volume, access, stairs/elevators, parking, and disposal requirements before work begins.
Protect routine traffic areas and surrounding surfaces where practical during removal.
Load, haul, and dispose of agreed non-hazardous items according to the project details.
Separate donation, recycling, and disposal items where practical and agreed.
Sweep or tidy the immediate cleared area after routine removal work.
Confirm completion with the client before leaving the site.
paymentTerms: Payment is due on completion unless commercial or recurring terms are agreed.
A deposit may be requested for large cleanouts, reserved crew time, or disposal-heavy jobs.
Additional volume, weight, labor, or restricted disposal items may be quoted separately.
assumptions: Quote assumes safe access and non-hazardous items unless stated in the project details.
Paint, chemicals, asbestos, biohazards, fuel, medical waste, and other regulated materials are excluded unless agreed separately.
Final pricing may change if item volume, weight, access, or disposal requirements differ from the details provided.
Client will identify items to remain and secure valuables before removal begins.
